analysis.note: External sandhi: vipākaḥ + manana- → vipāko manana-. Taken as a masculine nominal technical term, not as an adjective.
External sandhi: mananākhyaḥ + ca → mananākhyaś ca. The compound means “called/named manana.”

verification.detail: compound stem mananākhya as Pum/Prathama/Eka: pass; relation bahuvrihi: unsupported (logged)

analysis.note: External sandhi: vijñaptiḥ + viṣayasya → vijñaptir viṣayasya.
Genitive dependent on vijñaptiḥ: “presentation/representation of an object-domain.”
Coordinates vijñaptiḥ viṣayasya with the preceding listed items.
External sandhi group: tatra + ālayākhyam → tatrālayākhyaṃ; surface_in_sandhi records the coalesced tatrā-. Interpreted resumptively, “among these.”
Neuter nominative agreeing with vijñānam. Final -m is printed as anusvāra before v in the verse.

verification.detail: compound stem ālayākhya as Napumsaka/Prathama/Eka: pass; relation bahuvrihi: unsupported (logged)

analysis.note: Neuter nominative in the nominal sentence; subject/appositional phrase with ālayākhyam and sarvabījakam.
Predicate nominal: “is the maturation-result.” The masculine technical noun is predicated of the neuter vijñānam phrase.
Neuter nominative agreeing with vijñānam: “having all seeds / all-seeded.”

verification.detail: compound stem sarvabījaka as Napumsaka/Prathama/Eka: pass; relation bahuvrihi: unsupported (logged)

justifications.decision: The repeated ca and the sequence of nominative expressions support an enumerative construal: “the maturation-result, the one called mentation, and the representation of an object.” I do not take mananākhyaḥ merely as an adjective qualifying vipākaḥ.
options_considered: Three coordinated items: vipākaḥ; mananākhyaḥ; vijñaptiḥ viṣayasya
Two items, with mananākhyaḥ qualifying vipākaḥ
justifications.chosen: The first half lists three items: vipākaḥ, mananākhyaḥ, and viṣayasya vijñaptiḥ.

evidence.quote: [MW s.v. ca, 380,1] 2. ca ¦ ind. and, both, also, moreover, as well as
evidence.translation: The enclitic ca supplies coordination; repeated ca marks a list.

justifications.decision: The element -ākhya is best understood through ākhyā “appellation, name.” Since the whole form functions adjectivally, agreeing with an expressed or implied noun, the analysis is bahuvrīhi: manana-ākhyā yasya saḥ; ālaya-ākhyā yasya tat.
options_considered: Uncompounded sequence manana ākhyā / ālaya ākhyā
justifications.chosen: mananākhyaḥ and ālayākhyam are bahuvrīhi compounds meaning “whose name/appellation is X,” hence “called X.”

evidence.quote: [MW s.v. ākhyā, 129,1] 2. ā-khyā ¦ f. ... appellation, name
evidence.translation: The lexical sense “name/appellation” supports the analysis “called/named X.”

evidence.quote: [MW s.v. manana, 783,3] mana/na ¦ n. thinking, reflection, meditation, thought
evidence.translation: manana is the name by which the second listed item is called.

evidence.quote: [MW s.v. ālaya, 154,1] ā-laya ¦ m. and n. a house, dwelling ... a receptacle, asylum
evidence.translation: ālaya is the name by which the vijñāna is called.

justifications.decision: Although tatra can mean “there,” its locative-pronominal force also permits a resumptive sense after an enumeration. Here it introduces the definition of vipākaḥ from among the listed terms.
justifications.chosen: tatra is read as “among these,” referring back to the listed items.

evidence.quote: [MW s.v. tatra, 433,2] ta/tra ¦ ind. ... used for the loc. ... of ta/d ... in that place, there
evidence.translation: As a locative adverb of tad, tatra can refer back to the just-mentioned set: “in/among those.”

justifications.decision: The second half is a nominal sentence without an expressed verb. The neuter nominatives ālayākhyam, vijñānam, and sarvabījakam hang together by agreement, while vipākaḥ supplies the technical label: “the maturation-result is consciousness called ālaya, all-seeded.” The gender mismatch is due to predicating a masculine noun-label of a neuter noun phrase.
options_considered: Neuter subject phrase + masculine predicate noun
All words in apposition requiring same gender
ālayākhyaṃ vijñānaṃ as accusative object of an omitted verb
justifications.chosen: ālayākhyaṃ vijñānaṃ sarvabījakam is the neuter nominative subject phrase; vipākaḥ is a masculine predicate noun.

evidence.translation: Grammatical reasoning: neuter nominative singular ālayākhyam and sarvabījakam agree with neuter vijñānam; vipākaḥ is nominative masculine and can function as a predicate nominal in an equational sentence.

justifications.decision: Since sarvabījakam is neuter nominative singular, it naturally agrees with vijñānam. The compound is better read possessively than as an independent noun: sarvāṇi bījakāni yasya tat.
justifications.chosen: sarvabījakam is a bahuvrīhi adjective, “having all seeds / all-seeded,” modifying vijñānam.

evidence.quote: [MW s.v. sarva, 1184,3] sa/rva ¦ mf(ā)n. ... whole, entire, all, every

evidence.translation: bījaka supplies “seed”; the compound yields “having all seeds.”

justifications.decision: MW records vijñapti as “information/report” and “imparting,” while viṣaya supplies the object-domain or sphere. Although vijñapti can take a genitive in the sense “request of,” that sense is semantically unsuitable with viṣaya here; an objective genitive, “presentation of the object,” is the better grammatical-lexical construal.
options_considered: Genitive with vijñapti as “request/entreaty of the object”
justifications.chosen: viṣayasya is an objective genitive with vijñaptiḥ: “representation/presentation of an object.”

evidence.quote: [MW s.v. vijñapti, 961,1] vi-˚jñapti ¦ f. information, report ... imparting, giving
evidence.translation: The lexical range supports “making known / presentation / representation.”

evidence.quote: [MW s.v. viṣaya, 997,1] viṣaya ¦ m. ... scope, compass, horizon, range, reach (of eyes, ears, mind &c.)
evidence.translation: viṣaya can denote the range or object-domain of cognition.

translation:
[The three are:] the maturation-result (vipāka),
the one called mentation (manana),
and the representation (vijñapti) of an object (viṣaya).
Among these, the maturation-result is consciousness (vijñāna),
called the repository (ālaya), all-seeded (sarvabījaka).
one_shot_delta: Without commentary, the exact doctrinal identities of the three listed items remain inferred from grammar and context rather than demonstrated: vipākaḥ, mananākhyaḥ, and viṣayasya vijñaptiḥ are translated as three coordinated terms.

A dictionary-only reader might take vijñaptiḥ viṣayasya as “request of the object,” because MW records vijñapti with genitive in the sense of entreaty; the semantic context makes “representation/presentation of an object” preferable.
A purely spatial rendering of tatra as “there” would miss the likely resumptive force “among these” after the enumeration.
sarvabījakam could be flattened into “all seed”; the agreement with vijñānam and the compound idiom favor “all-seeded / having all seeds.”
analyzer_disagreements: No substantive grammatical disagreement with the ByT5 output. The analyzer reports compounds by their members (manana + ākhyā, ālaya + ākhyā, sarva + bījaka); this apparatus reports the required whole compound stems as the lemmas: mananākhya, ālayākhya, sarvabījaka.
The neuter forms ālayākhyam, vijñānam, and sarvabījakam are taken as nominatives by nominal-clause syntax, not as accusatives.
open_questions: The precise technical force of vipāka, vijñapti, and sarvabījaka in this śāstra cannot be fully fixed from MW and grammar alone.
The commentary would be needed to determine whether mananākhyaḥ specifically denotes a technical mental faculty or only “the one called thinking/mentation.”
The translation “representation” for vijñapti is context-sensitive; MW alone gives more general senses such as “information,” “report,” and “imparting.”
